summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to O'Neil's Bloomberg View column criticizing the Gates Foundation's education reform efforts. The post frames the foundation's big-data approach to improving public schools as not merely unsuccessful but actively harmful. O'Neil signals her core argument through the column's subtitle: that well-intentioned data-driven reforms can cause real damage when implemented poorly.
key_points: The Gates Foundation's education reform initiative is characterized as a failure
The critique goes beyond ineffectiveness to claim the reforms caused actual harm
Big-data approaches to education reform are implicitly questioned
money_quotes: The Gates Foundation's big-data experiment wasn't just a failure. It did real harm.
key_insight: Data-driven education reform, even when backed by enormous resources and good intentions, can cause real harm when it treats students and teachers as optimization problems.
